title: Oracle Cloud Fusion P2P and O2C Cycle Audit Comprehensive Practice Set
description: An exhaustive, high-difficulty multiple-choice question set evaluating technical navigation, auditing risks, transaction flows, and operational reports for the Procure-to-Pay (P2P) and Order-to-Cash (O2C) cycles within Oracle Cloud Fusion.

questions.question: Which of the following operational vulnerabilities represents an audit risk directly associated with the P2P cycle due to its multi-process and multi-person nature, despite the presence of automated ERP process controls?
options: Invoices being processed and matched solely via manual ledger integrations without system interference.
Executing payments to suppliers without an official verification of actual physical delivery of goods.
Sales orders remaining in an 'awaiting shipping' status due to credit check holds.
Importing receivables transactions into the ledger using automated third-party auto-invoice utilities.
questions.explanation: According to Section 1.1 of the text, procurement involves complex operations with a high amount of money outflow. Key audit risks explicitly enumerated include 'Payments without delivery of goods' and transactions without proper approvals, which justifies the need for an audit even when ERP solutions offer process automation.

questions.question: An auditor is reviewing the system trail for the initiation of an internal procurement request for an unlisted corporate asset. What initial navigation flow must a user execute in Oracle Cloud Fusion to begin creating a Purchase Requisition for a non-catalog item?
options: Procurement > My Receipt > More Tasks > Request Noncatalog Item
Payables > Invoices > Create Invoice > Request Noncatalog Item
Procurement > Purchase Requisition > More Tasks > Request Noncatalog Item
Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management > Requisitions > Request Noncatalog Item
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.1 specifies that to create a Purchase Requisition, the user must navigate to 'Procurement > Purchase requisition', click the 'More Tasks' dropdown, and select 'Request Noncatalog Item'.

questions.question: During the P2P cycle in Oracle Cloud Fusion, what action must the user take immediately after entering the non-catalog requisition details to proceed with reviewing the item in their shopping interface?
options: Click on the 'Add to Document Builders' button.
Click on the 'Add to Cart' button followed by clicking on the Cart and selecting 'Review'.
Click 'Submit for Manager Approval' directly from the non-catalog item screen.
Navigate to 'Payable > Invoices' to automatically associate the line item.
questions.explanation: As detailed in Section 1.2.1 (steps iii, v, and vi), the user enters the requisition details, clicks the 'Add to Cart' button, clicks on the Cart icon, selects the 'Review' button, and finally clicks 'Submit'.

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes the structured components and function of a Purchase Order (PO) as defined within Oracle Cloud Fusion Purchasing operations?
options: A PO is an internal-only financial ledger account that cannot utilize data retrieved from the Supplier Item Catalogue.
A PO is broken down into portions called PO header and PO lines, defining sale terms, costs, quantities, and need-by dates.
A PO is generated exclusively after a payable invoice has been validated and matched to a delivery receipt.
A PO acts as a customer billing record detailing the ship-from warehouse location and tax-exempt statuses.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.2 establishes that a Purchase Order is a legal document defining sale terms, costs, quantities, and need-by dates. It notes that it can utilize the Supplier Item Catalogue form to retrieve quotation information and is broken down into PO header and PO lines.

questions.question: When converting an approved Purchase Requisition into a valid Purchase Order in Oracle Cloud Fusion, what sequence of interface actions must be performed by the purchasing agent within the 'Process Requisition' screen?
options: Search by requisition number, click 'Review', then directly click 'Post to Ledger'.
Select the requisition line, click 'Add to Document Builders', select the line in the builder interface, and click 'Create'.
Navigate to 'My Receipt', choose the requisition line, and select 'Ship Confirm'.
Open 'Manage Invoices', input the requisition ID, and select the 'Match Invoice Line' button.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.2 (steps i to iv) outlines that the user must click on the 'Process Requisition' task, search and select the requisition, click 'Add to Document Builders', select the line, and click the 'Create' button to generate the order.

questions.question: What is the automatic system consequence immediately following the final submission of a newly generated Purchase Order number by a procurement specialist?
options: The system routes the document to 'Payables > Invoices' for automatic checkout.
The system generates an automatic receipt number and updates the warehouse inventory balances.
The document is submitted to the system for manager approval.
The system triggers the 'Import Receivables Transactions Using Autoinvoice' process.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.2 (step vii) explicitly states that after the user verifies the details and clicks the submit button, 'It will submit for manager approval.'

questions.question: In Oracle Cloud Fusion, how is the physical arrival of ordered commodities officially recorded and verified against the initial purchase terms within the system?
options: By navigating to 'Receivables > Billing' and matching the invoice line to the packing slip.
By navigating to 'Procurement > My Receipt', searching for the PO or requisition number, selecting the line, clicking 'Receive', and entering the approved quantity.
By executing the 'Print Pick Slip Report' within the Period Close work area.
By opening the 'Inquire on Detail Balances' dashboard and exporting the subledger transactions to Excel.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.3 explains that the receipt is completed by navigating to 'Procurement > My Receipt', searching for the requisition or PO number, selecting the line, clicking 'Receive', and entering the receive quantity.

questions.question: An accounting clerk is entering a vendor invoice into Oracle Cloud Fusion. What navigation path and subsequent matching steps are required to ensure the invoice line item references a valid procurement foundation?
options: Navigate to 'Procurement > Purchase Requisitions', enter the invoice number, and click 'Validate'.
Navigate to 'Payables > Invoices', select 'Create Invoice', search with the PO number, select the match invoice line, click 'GO', select the PO, and click 'Apply'.
Navigate to 'Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management', click 'Manage Shipments', and input the PO reference.
Navigate to 'General Accounting Dashboard > Period Close', select 'Inquire on Detail Balances', and check the PO status.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.4 details the exact sequence: Navigate to 'Payables > Invoices', click 'Create Invoice', search with the PO number, enter invoice details, select the match invoice line, click 'GO', select the PO, and click 'Apply, OK'.

questions.question: To finalize an invoice's readiness for accounting distribution after its lines have defaulted from a matched Purchase Order, what administrative action sequence must be executed within the invoice interface?
options: Click on Invoice Actions > Post to Ledger.
Click on Invoice Actions > Validate.
Click on More Tasks > Request Noncatalog Item.
Click on Actions > View Accounting > Save and Close.
questions.explanation: According to Section 1.2.4 (step v), after the PO lines default into the invoice lines, the user must validate the invoice by clicking on 'Invoice Actions > Validate'.

questions.question: An internal auditor needs to pull up an existing invoice to verify its systemic status. Which navigation route should they utilize to look up specific invoice records using an invoice number query?
options: Procurement > Purchase Requisition > Review Requisition
Receivables > Accounts Receivables > Manage Receipts
Payables > Invoices > Manage Invoices
General Accounting > General Accounting Dashboard > Inquire on Detail Balances
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.5 explicitly specifies the navigation path for searching invoices as: 'Payables > Invoices > Manage Invoices', followed by entering the invoice number and clicking search.

questions.question: What parameters and forms must be populated to close out the P2P cycle when issuing a settlement to a vendor via the Oracle Cloud Fusion payment interface?
options: Navigate to 'Payables > Payments', click 'Create Payment', input mandatory information, search for and apply the targeted invoice number, then save and close.
Navigate to 'Receivables > Billing', click 'Create Receipt', input the customer's sales order number, and click 'Post to Ledger'.
Navigate to 'Supply Chain Execution', click 'Create Pick Wave', reference the PO number, and click 'Release Now'.
Navigate to 'Procurement > My Receipt', click 'Create Payment Link', input the distribution account, and click 'Submit'.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.6 details the concluding step of the P2P cycle: Navigate to 'Payables > Payments', click 'Create Payment', enter mandatory information, search and apply the invoice number, then save and close.

questions.question: Following a confirmed supplier payment, an auditor wishes to inspect the balanced debit and credit entries ledger distribution directly from the payment interface. What menu action must be invoked?
options: Invoice Actions > Validate
Actions > Post to Ledger
Actions > View Accounting
Tasks > Inquire on Detail Balances
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.6 (step vii) shows that to view the payment accounting entry within the payment interface, the user must navigate to 'Actions > View Accounting'.

questions.question: Within the 'Manage Payments' window, what specific functionality is available to users who wish to inspect underlying transaction origins and allocation streams directly from a payment search record?
options: Users can drill down to the invoice and distribution details by clicking on the invoice number under the 'Paid Invoices' tab.
Users can modify the approved quantity listed on the original warehouse receipt.
Users can execute an 'Import Receivables Transactions Using Autoinvoice' routine.
Users can re-route the payment to a non-catalog requisition profile.
questions.explanation: Section 1.3 states that users can search by payment number or supplier name, click on the invoice number under the Paid Invoices tab, and 'drilldown to the invoice and distribution from the payment screen.'

questions.question: A financial manager requires a breakdown of outstanding corporate liabilities categorized into custom-configured chronological intervals. Which report should be executed within Oracle's P2P reporting framework?
options: Payables Invoice Register
Supplier Balance Aging Report
Collections Aging 4 Bucket Report
Print Pick Slip Report
questions.explanation: Section 1.4.1 explains that the 'Supplier Balance Aging Report' is used to view supplier balances according to specified aging periods and provides a breakdown across aging buckets configured by the user.

questions.question: The 'Payables Invoice Register' can be filtered and executed by an auditor based on which criteria to review historical subledger transaction criteria?
options: Warehouse subinventory, staging locator, and carrier method.
Customer number, shipping priority, and outstanding receivables currency.
Specific invoice group, date range, accounting period, and statuses such as validated or canceled.
Pick wave number, movement request lines, and customer balance parameters.
questions.explanation: Section 1.4.2 states that the 'Payables Invoice Register' provides detailed information about invoices and can be run for a specific invoice group, date range, accounting period, and status (such as validated or canceled).

questions.question: Which of the following correct summaries delineates the formal boundaries and objective of the Order-to-Cash (O2C) cycle as handled by corporate audit review parameters?
options: It begins with the creation of a non-catalog purchase item request and ends with vendor payment reconciliation.
It represents the flow of revenue into the company, starting when a customer places an order and ending when payment is received and the order is fulfilled.
It manages internal asset transfers between the Exocore Inv Org warehouse and ledger subinventories.
It details the verification of unpaid supplier balances across historical aging buckets.
questions.explanation: Section 2.1 defines the Order-to-Cash (OTC) process as the set of activities that begin when a customer places an order and ends when payment is received and the order is fulfilled, representing the flow of revenue.

questions.question: What is the initial systemic path required to enter a newly received customer request into the Oracle Cloud Fusion sales pipeline?
options: Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management > Tasks > Create Sales Order
Receivables > Billing > Manage Transactions > Create Order
Order Management > Order Management > Create Order button
General Accounting Dashboard > Period Close > Inquire on Detail Balances
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.1 outlines the first stage of the O2C process: Navigate to 'Order Management > Order Management' and click on the 'Create Order' button.

questions.question: During the manual entry of a Sales Order, where must the operations clerk specify the physical storage facility node responsible for order provisioning, and where do they verify billing terms?
options: Warehouse details are provided in the Supply Tab; payment terms and Bill-to address are under the Billing and Payment Details.
Warehouse details default into the Document Builders; billing addresses are updated under the Paid Invoices tab.
Warehouse details are input via the Request Noncatalog Item dropdown; billing parameters are pulled from the Supplier Balance Aging interface.
Warehouse details are selected during Confirm Pick Slips; payment terms are established in the Post to Ledger screen.
questions.explanation: According to Section 2.2.1, the user must provide warehouse details in the 'Supply Tab' (step v) and provide payment terms and the Bill-to address under 'Billing and Payment Details' (steps vi and vii).

questions.question: When an analyst monitors a newly submitted Sales Order, what progressive line status changes occur immediately after clicking 'Submit' and then clicking 'Refresh'?
options: The status moves from 'Staged' to 'Interfaced'.
The status moves from 'Processing' to 'Awaiting Shipping'.
The status moves from 'Validated' to 'Awaiting Billing'.
The status moves from 'Open' to 'Post to Ledger'.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.1 (steps ix and ixx) states that upon submission, the order status will be 'processing', and clicking the Refresh button changes the order line status to 'awaiting shipping'.

questions.question: Once a Sales Order line transitions to 'Awaiting Shipping', what subsequent warehouse administrative step must be initiated to authorize inventory retrieval operations?
options: The warehouse team must run the 'Import Receivables Transactions Using Autoinvoice' process.
The warehouse operations must navigate to Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management and submit the order for a Pick Wave via 'Create Pick Wave'.
The clerk must create an accounting entry by choosing 'Actions > Post to Ledger'.
The system must match the order line to a pre-existing Supplier Balance Aging period.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.2 dictates that once line status changes to 'Awaiting Shipping', operations move to 'Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management' and select the Picks task to 'Create Pick Wave'.

questions.question: To execute a Pick Wave release for a targeted order within the Inventory Management interface, what specific parameters must be chosen or input by the warehouse clerk?
options: The unique invoice number, supplier name, and distribution account line numbers.
The unique requisition number, non-catalog item type, and payment method details.
The Organization name and the specific Sales Order Number, followed by clicking the 'Release Now' button.
The customer outstanding balances, aging periods, and ledger period activity month.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.2 (steps iii and iv) shows that the user must select the 'Organization name', enter the 'Order Number', and click the 'Release Now' button.

questions.question: Which document job is automatically submitted by Oracle Cloud Fusion immediately following the successful generation of a Pick Wave number?
options: Payables Invoice Register
Print Packing Slip Report
Print Pick Slip Report
Supplier Balance Aging Report
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.2 (step vi) states explicitly that after the Pick Wave number is generated, the 'Print Pick Slip Report job will be submitted automatically.'

questions.question: Based on the provided example data for Pick Slip number 353012, which physical warehouse location is designated as the source subinventory and locator for retrieving the product 'Nylon Thread'?
options: Subinventory EXASTORES, Locator EXARM
Subinventory EXARM, Locator ROW 1 RACK B
Subinventory NASAMUKK, Locator EXA PICK GRP RULE
Subinventory New York, Locator RACK B
questions.explanation: The Pick Slip table in Section 2.2.2 lists the Pick-from Subinventory as 'EXARM' and under the item line details for 'NYLON THREAD', the Pick-from Locator is specified as 'ROW 1 RACK B'.

questions.question: What is the correct navigation and operational sequence required to officially confirm that ordered items have been picked from warehouse shelves?
options: Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management > Picks task > Confirm Pick Slips option > Search with Order number > select Pick Slip number > Select line > Confirm.
Order Management > Order Management > Refresh > Action > Confirm Pick Slips > Post to Ledger.
Receivables > Accounts Receivables > Create Receipt > Add Open Receivables > Select Pick Line > Done.
Payables > Invoices > Manage Invoices > Actions > Validate Pick Slip Line.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.3 (steps i to iv) outlines this exact procedural path under 'Create Pick Confirm' using the Inventory Management work area.

questions.question: Immediately following a successful Pick Confirmation but prior to dispatch confirmation, what statuses will be observed on the shipment line and integration parameters within 'Manage Shipments'?
options: Shipment line status is 'Awaiting Billing'; integration status is 'Interfaced'.
Shipment line status is 'Staged'; integration status is 'Ready to Interface'.
Shipment line status is 'Processing'; integration status is 'Awaiting Shipping'.
Shipment line status is 'Complete'; integration status is 'Post to Ledger'.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.4 (steps iv and v) specifies that when searching the shipment number, the 'Shipment line status is changed to Staged' and the 'shipment line integration status is Ready to Interface'.

questions.question: To finalize dispatch and interface shipment data outwards, what operational data must be provided by the warehouse clerk in the shipment details interface before clicking 'Ship Confirm'?
options: The distribution account number and the aging period bucket.
The customer receipt method and the invoice currency rate.
The shipping method, alongside selecting the specific shipment line.
The non-catalog item code and manager approval token.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.4 (steps vi and vii) states that the user must 'Provide the shipping method and select the shipment line' before clicking the 'ship confirm' button.

questions.question: What concurrent system status changes happen to a fulfillment line immediately after the user executes the 'Ship Confirm' action for an active warehouse freight package?
options: Shipment line status becomes 'Interfaced' and the overarching Order line status transitions to 'Awaiting Billing'.
Shipment line status becomes 'Staged' and the Order line status transitions to 'Processing'.
Shipment line status becomes 'Awaiting Shipping' and the Order line status transitions to 'Complete'.
Shipment line status becomes 'Ready to Interface' and the Order line status transitions to 'Awaiting Billing'.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.4 (steps viii and ix) clarifies that after clicking ship confirm, the 'Shipment line status will changed to Interfaced' and the 'Order line status is changed to awaiting billing'.

questions.question: Which internal document is generated to accompany a physical warehouse shipment to a client, specifying details like Shipped Quantity and Shipping Method (e.g., Fed EX-Truck-Parcel), as demonstrated in the Chapter 2 case study?
options: Pick Slip Report
Packing Slip (via Print Packing Slip Report)
Payables Invoice Register
Supplier Balance Aging Document
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.4 (step x) outlines running the 'Print Packing Slip Report', which produces the 'Packing Slip' containing fields for initial ship date, shipping method, and a table of requested vs shipped quantities.

questions.question: What is the precise name of the scheduled system utility that must be executed to programmatically transform completed fulfillment transaction flows into official customer account billings?
options: Inquire on Detail Balances Process
Import Receivables Transactions Using Autoinvoice
Post to Ledger Billing Integration
Supplier Balance Auto-Match Job
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.4 (step xi) dictates that to interface the shipping data into billing, the user must run the job titled 'Import Receivables Transactions Using Autoinvoice'.

questions.question: An execution report summary for an Autoinvoice run indicates that 1 line was rejected, showing a value of 15.00 under the 'Invoice Currency Amount' column. In a real-world audit context, what does this report signal?
options: A vendor invoice was successfully paid out via the Payables dashboard.
An error or rejection occurred preventing that specific transaction line from successfully importing as a standard invoice.
A pick slip grouping rule was violated during warehouse pick wave distribution.
The general ledger period activity balances match perfectly to Excel worksheets.
questions.explanation: The table in Section 2.2.4 (step xii) titled 'Rejected Lines by Currency' and 'Lines with Errors by Currency' details transaction counts that failed or were rejected during the import process, isolating the errored amounts (such as USD 15.00).

questions.question: To verify that an autoinvoiced customer bill has been successfully generated with a binding completion status, an auditor must navigate to which modules and view which parameters?
options: Payables > Invoices > Manage Invoices, checking for a 'Validated' label.
Receivables > Billing > Manage Transactions, verifying that the Transaction Number displays a 'Complete' status.
Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management, verifying an 'Interfaced' shipment state.
General Accounting Dashboard > Period Close, checking for an excel sheet export tag.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.5 (steps i to iv) outlines navigating to 'Receivables > Billing', selecting 'Manage Transactions', searching the record, and verifying that the 'Transaction Number is with Complete status'.

questions.question: An auditor working inside the 'Manage Transactions' view wants to analyze the historical statement of customer commitments vs offsets for a chosen record. Which UI navigation path provides this balance summary?
options: More Tasks > Request Noncatalog Item
Invoice Actions > Validate Balance
Actions menu > View Balance Details
Actions menu > View Accounting
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.5 (step v) shows that to check balance breakdowns, the user must click on 'Actions and Click on View Balance Details'.

questions.question: Before an accounting journal entry for a customer billing transaction can be viewed or analyzed via subledger accounting interfaces, what action must first be taken by the specialist?
options: The transaction must be released via a Pick Wave number.
The specialist must select Actions > Post to Ledger.
The specialist must navigate to Payables > Payments and run a Supplier Aging Report.
The transaction lines must be deleted and re-imported via manual data entry.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.5 (steps vi and vii) explicitly shows that to create accounting for the transaction, the user must go to 'Actions > Post to Ledger' before they can utilize 'Action > View Accounting'.

questions.question: What is the initial systemic navigation trail required to log an inbound customer remittance into the accounts receivable ledger module?
options: Receivables > Accounts Receivables > Receipts Task > Create Receipt
Payables > Payments > Create Payment
Order Management > Order Management > Create Order
Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management > Confirm Pick Slips
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.6 (steps i and ii) establishes that to create a receipt and apply it, the user goes to 'Receivables > Accounts Receivables' and selects 'Create Receipt' from the Receipts Task menu.

questions.question: When configuring a manual cash receipt within the accounts receivable subledger, which primary metadata items must be hardcoded as mandatory details before searching for open bills?
options: Ship-to location attention headers, carrier codes, and subinventory rows.
Receipt Method, a unique Receipt Number, and the specific Receipt Amount.
Supplier Balance Aging buckets, invoice distribution lines, and non-catalog tags.
Purchase Order headers, requisition line item values, and manager approval codes.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.6 (step iii) shows that within the Create Receipt form, the user must explicitly 'Enter the Receipt Method, unique Receipt Number and Receipt Amount'.

questions.question: To attach an unapplied cash receipt to a specific outstanding customer invoice statement within the Oracle Cloud Fusion interface, what command button sequence must be triggered?
options: Click 'Add to Document Builders', select the line, and click 'Create Order'.
Click 'Add Open Receivables', search for the transaction or customer, select the line, click 'Add > Done', then click 'Submit and Apply Manually'.
Click 'Invoice Actions', select 'Validate', and then click 'Post to Ledger'.
Click 'More Tasks', choose 'Request Noncatalog Item', and select 'Save and Close'.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.6 (steps iv to viii) describes the complete operation: Click 'Add Open Receivables', search the transaction, select the line, click 'Add > Done', and then click 'Submit and Apply Manually'.

questions.question: What final steps must an accountant take after applying a receipt to save the changes and generate the corresponding balanced ledger distribution logs for that receipt?
options: Click on 'Refresh', then choose 'Invoice Actions > Validate'.
Click on 'Save', then choose Actions > Post to Ledger followed by Actions > View Accounting.
Click on 'Release Now', then republish the Print Pick Slip Report.
Click on 'Add to Cart', then navigate to 'Manage Invoices' to query the number.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.6 (steps ix, x, and xi) lists the concluding flow for receipt processing: Click 'Save', execute 'Actions > Post to Ledger', and view the entry via 'Actions > View Accounting'.

questions.question: An auditor is evaluating credit management efficiency and collection tracking methods. According to the text, how can credit teams use the 'Collections Aging 4 Bucket Report' to enhance operations?
options: It helps them re-route blocked sales orders to alternate warehouse subinventories like EXARM.
It lists customer's open transactions information based on defined aging buckets, which helps in creating collection strategies and tasks.
It isolates standard invoice types from standard item line descriptions to process manager approvals.
It automatically posts outstanding balances to the general ledger through an autoinvoice pipeline.
questions.explanation: Section 2.3.1 explicitly notes that the 'Collections Aging 4 Bucket Report' allows users to list customer's open transactions information, which can assist in 'creating collection strategies and tasks' based on defined aging buckets.

questions.question: In the provided example of the 'Aging - 4 Bucket Report' dated 7/9/14, which customer displays the largest outstanding balance profile, and what percentage of that balance is critically past due in the final bucket?
options: Imaging Innovations, Inc. with a balance of 9,927,707.71 and 0.00% past due.
UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC with a balance of 673,823.45 and 50.00% past due.
A T & T SOLUTIONS INC with an outstanding balance of 998,386,603,307.11 and 100.00% past due in the final bucket.
Vision Operations (USA) with a balance of 7,452.25 and 10.00% past due.
questions.explanation: The report data table in Section 2.3.1 outlines that 'A T & T SOLUTIONS INC' has an outstanding balance of '998,386,603,307.11' and shows '100.00%' allocated under the final aging column bucket.

questions.question: Where is the 'Inquire on Detail Balances' task located within Oracle Cloud Fusion, and what primary business area governs this function?
options: It is located under Payables > Invoices within the Procurement work area.
It is accessed by selecting the task in the Period Close work area via Home > General Accounting > General Accounting Dashboard.
It is located inside Supply Chain Execution > Inventory Management under the Picks task area.
It is reached via Order Management > Billing and Payment details configuration screen.
questions.explanation: Section 2.4 specifies that to perform account inquiries, the user must navigate to 'Home > General Accounting > General Accounting Dashboard' and select the 'Inquire on Detail Balances' task in the 'Period Close' work area.

questions.question: What explicit analytical utility does the 'Inquire on Detail Balances' screen offer to an auditor examining actual balances during year-end closings?
options: It allows them to generate automated pick slips and change warehouse allocation numbers.
It enables them to drill to journal lines for actual balances and then drill down further to the journal entry or subledger transactions, plus export balances to Excel.
It allows them to match supplier invoices to receiving forms without PO validation protocols.
It allows them to update the ship-to location addresses on historical packing slips.
questions.explanation: Section 2.4 lists the exact capabilities of the Account Inquiry feature: 'Drill to journal lines for actual balances and then drill to the journal entry or subledger transactions' and 'Export the balances to Excel'.

questions.question: To target specific financial transaction details inside the 'Inquire on Detail Balances' dashboard interface, what filtering metrics must be supplied, and what must be clicked to examine monthly details?
options: Enter the invoice type and supplier name, then click on the distribution line type.
Enter the unique customer receipt method and shipment set ID, then click on the packing slip header.
Enter the Accounting period and Account segment values, then click on the period activity for the required month within the search results.
Enter the Organization name and pick wave number, then click on the release now token.
questions.explanation: Section 2.4 (steps iii and iv) notes that the user must enter the 'Accounting period and Account segment values', execute search, and then 'click on the period activity for the required month to check the transactions in details'.

questions.question: Which of the following describes a potential breakdown in matching controls during the P2P cycle that an auditor must actively hunt for, as listed in Chapter 1?
options: Sales orders remaining stuck in an active processing status within Order Management.
Invoices which are not matched to a valid Purchase Order (PO) or Goods Receipt Note (GRN).
Pick Wave jobs automatically submitting the Print Pick Slip Report framework.
Accounts receivable receipts being applied manually to complete transaction headers.
questions.explanation: Section 1.1 explicitly names 'Invoices which are not matched to PO or GRN' as one of the key risk reasons why the P2P process requires systematic auditing.

questions.question: What granular information categories are found localized within a Purchase Order (PO) document structure beyond its split into header and lines?
options: Ship To address, supplier details, distribution accounts, and purchasing criteria.
Outstanding collections aging buckets, risk receipt percentages, and customer balance totals.
Staging subinventories, pick slips grouping rules, and movement request line status fields.
Autoinvoice currency rejection errors and ledger account period metrics.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.2 establishes that the PO contains structured records including 'Ship To address, supplier details, distribution accounts, and purchasing criteria.'

questions.question: When examining vendor fulfillment steps, what is the direct functional purpose of entering data into Oracle Purchasing's receiving form?
options: To establish a new non-catalog item pricing profile within the supplier item catalogue.
To record commodities arriving at the distribution center into the warehouse system and check that everything ordered has been delivered.
To bypass manager approval pathways for invoices matching historical aging buckets.
To initiate an export layout containing transaction ledger activities directly into Excel worksheets.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.3 describes that entering receiving data 'records commodities arriving at the distribution centre and entering them into the warehouse system' to make sure that everything ordered has been delivered.

questions.question: Reviewing the 'Payables Invoice Register' example layout for Brighton Construction Inc., how is standard item line distribution tracking organized?
options: By matching pick wave numbers to specific customer billing lines.
By tracking separate distribution numbers with distinct distribution types, account strings, amounts, and accounting dates.
By routing non-catalog line items directly to the General Accounting Dashboard.
By auditing the shipment set metrics against the initial warehouse inventory organization.
questions.explanation: The Payables Invoice Register data table in Section 1.4.2 clearly separates lines into Distribution Number, Distribution Type, Account string, Amount, Accounting Date, and Accounted status.

questions.question: During an O2C audit, a reference number generated in the 'Pick Slip Report' must be later used to perform which transaction lookup inside warehouse shipment tracking?
options: It acts as the Supplier Balance Aging token to clear outstanding balances.
It generates the Transaction Source key required to run the Autoinvoice tool.
The Shipment Number generated in the Pick Slip Report is utilized to search and locate the record within 'Manage Shipments'.
It serves as the payment number query parameter inside the Manage Payments screen.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.4 (step iii) notes that when managing shipments, the user must 'Search with Shipment Number... this number is generated in Pick Slip Report'.

questions.question: What is the systematic integration status label applied to an order shipment line when it has been successfully staged in warehouse operations but has not yet undergone final ship confirmation dispatch?
options: Interfaced
Ready to Interface
Awaiting Billing
Awaiting Shipping
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.4 (step v) shows that once a shipment line is staged, the accompanying integration status field displays 'Ready to Interface'.

questions.question: Based on the official Packing Slip metadata sample in Chapter 2, what exact location and entity fields are listed under the 'Step From' / ship-from parameters?
options: NASA COLONY, New York, United States
Exacore Inv Org, Indeevaram Infopark, Kallur Thekkummuri, Kerala, India
Brighton Construction Inc., Vision Operations (USA)
AT & T SOLUTIONS INC, Customer Number 1001
questions.explanation: The Packing Slip data layout in Section 2.2.4 shows under 'Step From' (Ship from): 'Exacore Inv Org, Indeevaram Infopark, Kallur Thekkummuri, Kerala 680306, India'.

questions.question: Why is auditing unauthorized transactions or non-matching documentation critical inside an enterprise system context according to the manual text guidelines?
options: Because automated ERP modules eliminate paper trails, meaning errors or missing verification steps must be audited and analyzed directly within the system databases.
Because manual ledger transfers automatically override system calculations during period close processing.
Because non-catalog purchasing routes do not require managers to validate distribution ledger lines.
Because warehouse operations require physical print slip jobs to update online customer balances.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2 explains that because ERPs eliminate associated paperwork, auditors must conduct analysis within the system itself to capture risks like transactions without proper approval or missing document matching hooks.
